items.title: Execution harness artifacts exist and stay structurally valid
items.description: The active plan, acceptance JSON, and progress handoff are all present and validated by script.

items.title: Deterministic init entrypoint exists
items.description: A new session can run one script to check required commands, validate docs, and print the current acceptance summary.

items.title: Smoke entrypoint validates the harness end to end
items.description: The smoke script validates shell syntax, both harness validation scripts, the DuckDB dev foundation, and the backend/frontend startup chain.

items.title: Core docs explain the long-running workflow
items.description: Harness and planning docs explain how acceptance, progress, init, smoke, and the completion gate fit together.

items.title: DuckDB-backed backend and frontend dev skeletons can start
items.description: QuantA has deterministic backend/frontend dev commands, runtime directory bootstrap, a local DuckDB-backed latest snapshot path, and a snapshot-driven workbench shell that can be exercised by smoke tests.
